name: Suspicious Creation of Get-Variable File
notes:
This rule detects suspicious creation of get-variable.exe file.

Covered from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: frack113
SIGMA Title: Suspicious Get-Variable.exe Creation
SIGMA Description: Get-Variable is a valid PowerShell cmdlet
    WindowsApps is by default in the path where PowerShell is executed.
    So when the Get-Variable command is issued on PowerShell execution, the system first looks for the Get-Variable executable in the path and executes the malicious binary instead of looking for the PowerShell cmdlet.


Confidence: LOW

This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event category for the event is one of the following System.File Created, System.Successful File Modification
when the event matches Filename (custom) is any of Get-Variable.exe
when the event matches LOWER("File Directory") LIKE '%Local\Microsoft\WindowsApps\%' AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY Suspicious Creation of Get-Variable File on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following System.File Created, System.Successful File Modification
AND when the event matches Filename (custom) is any of Get-Variable.exe
AND when the event matches LOWER("File Directory") LIKE '%Local\Microsoft\WindowsApps\%' AQL filter query
RED_list: Event name: Suspicious Creation of Get-Variable File
Event description: Detects suspicious creation of get-variable.exe file.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
